verses.text: The psalmist summons all peoples, high and low, to hear his wise 'dark saying upon the harp.' He has a lesson for everyone. The deepest truths about life and death concern rich and poor alike.
The psalmist summons all peoples, high and low, to hear his wise 'dark saying upon the harp.' He has a lesson for everyone. The deepest truths about life and death concern rich and poor alike.
The psalmist summons all peoples, high and low, to hear his wise 'dark saying upon the harp.' He has a lesson for everyone. The deepest truths about life and death concern rich and poor alike.
The psalmist summons all peoples, high and low, to hear his wise 'dark saying upon the harp.' He has a lesson for everyone. The deepest truths about life and death concern rich and poor alike.

Why fear in evil days, when those who trust their wealth 'none... can by any means redeem his brother,' for the soul's ransom is too costly? Money cannot buy life. No riches can purchase one more hour from death, or redeem a soul.

Why fear in evil days, when those who trust their wealth 'none... can by any means redeem his brother,' for the soul's ransom is too costly? Money cannot buy life. No riches can purchase one more hour from death, or redeem a soul.
Wise and foolish alike die and leave their wealth to others; 'man being in honour abideth not.' Death levels all. Earthly greatness, however dazzling, passes like that of the beasts.
Wise and foolish alike die and leave their wealth to others; 'man being in honour abideth not.' Death levels all. Earthly greatness, however dazzling, passes like that of the beasts.
Wise and foolish alike die and leave their wealth to others; 'man being in honour abideth not.' Death levels all. Earthly greatness, however dazzling, passes like that of the beasts.

Such is the folly of those who trust themselves, but 'God will redeem my soul from the power of the grave: for he shall receive me.' Here shines resurrection hope. What gold cannot do, God Himself does for His own.

Such is the folly of those who trust themselves, but 'God will redeem my soul from the power of the grave: for he shall receive me.' Here shines resurrection hope. What gold cannot do, God Himself does for His own.

Such is the folly of those who trust themselves, but 'God will redeem my soul from the power of the grave: for he shall receive me.' Here shines resurrection hope. What gold cannot do, God Himself does for His own.
'Be not thou afraid when one is made rich,' for at death 'he shall carry nothing away.' Envy of the wealthy is needless. They leave it all behind, and the grave makes no man rich.
'Be not thou afraid when one is made rich,' for at death 'he shall carry nothing away.' Envy of the wealthy is needless. They leave it all behind, and the grave makes no man rich.

Though he flattered himself while living, he joins his fathers in darkness; 'man that is in honour, and understandeth not, is like the beasts.' Honour without wisdom is empty. To live for this world only is to perish like a brute.
